name: Ix Tab
description: Ix Tab, also known as Ixtab, is the Mayan goddess of suicide. She is primarily associated with suicide by hanging, which was considered a noble and honorable death in Mayan culture, guaranteeing passage to paradise. Unlike the negative connotations often associated with suicide in other cultures, Ix Tab was viewed as a guide and protector for those who chose this path. She is often depicted with a rope around her neck, signifying the act of suicide, and with closed eyes, indicating death. Some accounts portray her as benevolent, escorting souls to the afterlife, while others suggest a more ambivalent role. Her presence highlights the Mayan understanding of death as a transition rather than an ending, and the acceptance of suicide under specific circumstances, such as sacrifice or escape from suffering. Ix Tab's role reflects the complex Mayan worldview, where death could be both tragic and honorable, and where deities played an active role in guiding souls through the afterlife. She embodies not only the act of self-destruction but also the promise of a better existence beyond the earthly realm. It is important to note that the Mayan view on suicide was nuanced and not universally accepted, with societal factors influencing its perception. Ix Tab's existence within the Mayan pantheon provides a unique insight into their spiritual beliefs and the acceptance of death as a part of life's cycle. Her influence is primarily seen in depictions of death and funerary practices, reflecting the belief in her role as a psychopomp for those who died by suicide.
subtitle: Mayan Goddess of Suicide by Hanging

keyMyths.title: The Rope's Embrace
keyMyths.description: Ix Tab is said to guide those who die by hanging to a special paradise. This act was considered honorable in certain Mayan contexts, ensuring a favorable afterlife.

keyMyths.title: The Guide to Paradise
keyMyths.description: Unlike other death deities who might be feared, Ix Tab is sometimes seen as a benevolent guide. She escorts souls to a better place after their earthly suffering.
keyMyths.source: Scholarly interpretations of Mayan funerary practices

keyMyths.title: Ambivalent Protector
keyMyths.description: While often seen as a guide, some interpretations suggest Ix Tab's role is more complex. She may not always offer salvation, reflecting the nuanced view of suicide in Mayan society.
keyMyths.source: Analysis of Mayan art and religious texts
